scope.summary: F-LOW AU2: CEF escaping let NEL, LINE SEPARATOR, PARAGRAPH SEPARATOR and the VT/FF controls through raw, so a collector could read one signed audit record as two; every record separator is now escaped in both the header and the extension.
out_of_scope: The syslog path, which already had a stronger physical-line backstop and is unchanged.
The JSON shipping format, which carries no line-delimited record framing of this kind.
The signed preimage and hash chain: this is a transport-encoding fix and changes no hashed bytes.

entry_points.name: cef_line / cef_escape_header / push_cef_kv
entry_points.trace: 241 oraclemcp-audit tests pass. Negative acceptance (no_record_separator_survives_into_a_cef_field): for each of LF, CR, VT, FF, U+0085, U+2028, U+2029 injected into a header field AND an extension field of a real signed record, the rendered CEF line contains no raw separator and stays exactly one physical line. Positive acceptance: 'é工具 ✅' round-trips unchanged through both escape paths, and the pre-existing delimiter escapes are asserted byte-identical, including the untouched cef_and_syslog_escaping_is_spec_specific test.

limitations: Discriminating, verified by mutation: restoring the CR/LF-only separator set fails both new tests.

The bead named five separators; VT (U+000B) and FF (U+000C) were included as well because they are the remaining Unicode mandatory line breaks and leaving them raw would reproduce the same defect under a different character. This is broader than the literal ask, and it changes the output only for values that already contained a control character.
Verified against synthetic records in-crate. No live SIEM ingestion was exercised.
Scoped verification: cargo test/clippy -p oraclemcp-audit. No workspace-wide run.
